revision.text:
[[ja:朝光寺原古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}; {{ill|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.54788888888889|N|139.54158333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Chōkōjibara Site|qid=Q88961983|1=Chōkōjibara Site}}}} [[File:朝光寺原古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts|qq=}}{{small|On display at the {{ill|Yokohama History Museum|en|Yokohama History Museum|qid=Q4902594|lt=Yokohama History Museum|1=Yokohama History Museum}}.|qq=}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label = Chōkōjihara
Kofun Cluster| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.|qq=}}|qq=}} '''Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster''' (朝光寺原古墳群, also pronounced Chōkōjibara Kofun Cluster) was a group of three ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Ichigao, Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|市ケ尾町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao, Aoba-ku, Yokohama|1=Ichigaochō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479968}}, {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|qid=Q904456|lt=Aoba-ku, Yokohama|1=Aoba-ku}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, on the east side of {{ill|Ichigao Station|en|Ichigao Station|qid=Q11479967|lt=Ichigao Station|1=Ichigao Station}}. The mounds were part of the {{ill|Chōkōjihara Ruins|ja|朝光寺原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōjihara Ruins|1=Chōkōjibara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q88961983}} but have since been completely destroyed. == Overview == {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|32|52.4|N|139|32|29.7|E|qq=}} | zoom = | float = right | width = 300| height =200 | caption = Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster (Location Reconstruction) | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|32|50.1|N|139|32|31.5|E|qq=}} | label1=Chōkōjihara No.1 Kofun | label-pos1=left | mark-title1=Chōkōjihara No.1 Kofun | label-size1= | label-color1=dark grey | mark-size1=10 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|32|55.5|N|139|32|27.3|E|qq=}} | label2= Chōkōjihara No.2 Kofun | label-pos2= left | mark-title2=Chōkōjihara No.2 Kofun | label-size2= | label-color2=dark grey | mark-size2=10 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|32|57.1|N|139|32|26.7|E|qq=}} | label3= Chōkōjihara No.3 Kofun | label-pos3=top | mark-title3=Chōkōjihara No.3 Kofun | label-size3= | label-color3=dark grey | mark-size3=10 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|32|55.2|N|139|32|36.2|E|qq=}} | label4=Archaeological Information Board | label-pos4=right | mark-title4=Archaeological Information Board | label-size4= | label-color4=dark grey | mark-size4=10 }} The Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster was located within the {{ill|Chōkōjihara Ruins|ja|朝光寺原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōjihara Ruins|1=Chōkōjibara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q88961983}}, situated north of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|qid=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River|1=Tsurumi River}}, also known as {{ill|Tanimoto River|ja|谷本川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanimoto River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鶴見川|1=Tsurumi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7850292}}, in a roughly **150-meter east-west** by **300-meter north-south** area between the {{ill|Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja|田園都市線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyū Den-en-toshi Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東急田園都市線|1=Den-en-toshi Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1190086}} and the {{ill|Tōmei Expressway|en|Tōmei Expressway|qid=Q1135694|lt=Tōmei Expressway|1=Tōmei Expressway}}, east of Ichigao Station. In the 1960s, during Japan’s **postwar economic boom**, rapid urban expansion occurred throughout Yokohama. In **1965** (Shōwa 40), the **Kōhoku 11 Town Project** was announced as part of the {{ill|Six Major Projects of Yokohama|ja|横浜市六大事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Major Projects of Yokohama|1=Six Major Projects of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11542991}}{{Sfn|Tanaka|2019|p=269|qq=}}. The following year, in **1966** (Shōwa 41), the **Den-en-toshi Line** opened, leading to extensive residential development in the then-{{ill|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1358965|lt=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}} and {{ill|Midori-ku|en|Midori-ku|qid=Q1140937|lt=Midori-ku, Yokohama|1=Midori-ku}} (now part of **Aoba-ku** and **Tsuzuki-ku**). This urbanization process involved flattening hills and filling valleys to create housing areas. Although Ichigao-chō was not part of Kōhoku 11 Town, the development of the Den-en-toshi Line resulted in **large-scale residential development**, replacing former farmland and forests with new neighborhoods{{Sfn|Yokohama History Museum|1998|pp=25-31|qq=}}. To protect archaeological sites from destruction, an **archaeological survey team** (Yokohama Northern Region Archaeological Investigation Committee) was formed, led by scholars such as **Ken Amakasu** and **Isamu Okamoto**. From **August 1 to December 10, 1967** (Shōwa 42), the **first excavation of the Chōkōjihara Ruins** took place, uncovering the Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster. The kofun cluster consisted of **three circular Kofuns**. Excavations revealed burial goods including: - **Armor** (Helmet with visor, **triangular-riveted tankō cuirass** – from Mound 1) - **Weapons** (Iron swords, iron halberds, iron knives, iron arrowheads) - **Horse gear** - **Jewelry** (Magatama, usutama [cylindrical beads]) Based on the artifacts, the mounds were built in the following order: 1. **Mound 1** (Late 5th century) 2. **Mound 2** (Around 6th century) 3. **Mound 3** (Early 6th century){{Sfn|Midori Ward, Yokohama|1985|p=75|qq=}}. The **armor set from Mound 1** (visor helmet and **triangular-riveted tankō cuirass**) remains **the only such discovery in Kanagawa Prefecture**, making it a significant find. == Present Status == After excavation, the site was **immediately destroyed**, and **all Kofuns were lost**. Today, an **informational sign about the Chōkōjihara Ruins** is located at **Ichigaochō Park**, although the park itself is **not within the original ruins area**. The **excavated artifacts**, including the **armor set from Mound 1**, are designated as **Yokohama City Cultural Properties** and are on display at the **Yokohama History Museum**{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/aoba/shokai/bunkazai/ta/bunkazai005.html|title=Excavated Artifacts from Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster|work=Aoba Ward, Yokohama|date=2018-10-23|accessdate=2020-03-22|qq=}}{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/koudou/see/kikakuten/2000/33/|title=Special Exhibition: Messages from Kofun - Yokohama's Kofuns and Grave Goods|work=Yokohama History Museum|date=March 2001|accessdate=2020-03-22|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == === Cited Works === *{{Cite book|ja|author=Midori Ward, Yokohama|title=History of Midori Ward, Yokohama|chapter=Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster|publisher=Midori Ward, Yokohama|year=1985|date=July 15, 1985|page=75|ncid=BN09184065|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|ja|author=Yokohama History Museum|chapter=Chapter 4: Excavations of the 1960s|title=Yokohama Archaeology: A Visual History of Excavations|publisher=Yokohama Furusato History Foundation|pages=25-31|year=1998|date=March 7, 1998|ncid=BA37874376|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|ja|last=Tanaka|first=Yoshiaki|title=Development and Archaeology: The Era of Ichigao Yokoana Cluster, Sandendai Ruins, and Inarimae Kofun Cluster|chapter=Chapter 4: Destroyed Sites and Transformed Regions|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2019|date=July 15, 2019|pages=285-404|ncid=BB28625447|isbn=9784787719096|ref=harv|qq=}} == External Links == *[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/aoba/shokai/bunkazai/ta/bunkazai005.html Excavated Artifacts from Chōkōjihara Kofun Cluster - Aoba Ward, Yokohama] {{Place name disambiguation|qq=}} * [[Inarimae Kofun Cluster]] ...Kofun Group|ja|朝光寺原古墳群|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ichigao Horizontal Tomb Group|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群|1=Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Horizontal Tomb Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}}, ... 937 bytes (105 words) - 21:54, 15 Inarimae Kofun Cluster ...er tumuli and horizontal tombs, such as the {{ill|Chokōji Kofun Group|ja|朝光寺原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokōji Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Ichigao Horizontal Tomb Group|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群|1=Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Horizontal Tomb Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}}, have been disc ... 12 KB (1,472 words) - 21:53, 15 2025 {{translated page|ja|朝光寺原古墳群|version=100588337|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:朝光寺原古墳群]] {{disambiguation}} {{wikidata link|Q97342105}} [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:横浜市の古墳]] [[Category:横浜市青葉区の歴史]]
